svg.title: Oceans Across — Secure Multi-Tenant Payroll Platform Architecture
svg.desc: AWS architecture diagram showing VPC with public and private subnets, three tenant EC2 instances, RDS PostgreSQL, S3 with KMS, IAM roles, Secrets Manager, NAT Gateway, and Internet Gateway.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do users connect to the Oceans Across payroll platform?')
sendPrompt('What does the Internet Gateway do in this architecture?')
sendPrompt('What role does the Bastion or ALB play in this payroll platform?')
sendPrompt('Why is a NAT Gateway used here, and where is it placed?')

g.text: Private subnets (AZ-a, AZ-b) — no public IPs

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the S3 Gateway Endpoint do and why is it used?')
sendPrompt('What does the Companies tenant EC2 instance handle?')
sendPrompt('What does the Bureaus tenant EC2 handle?')
sendPrompt('What does the Employees tenant EC2 handle?')

g.text: Security Groups: tenant-sg per instance · db-sg on RDS · port 5432 restricted

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How is the RDS PostgreSQL instance secured and isolated per tenant?')
sendPrompt('How does per-tenant IAM isolation work in this Terraform project?')
sendPrompt('What are instance profiles and how are they used here?')
sendPrompt('How are database credentials managed with Secrets Manager?')

g.text: Storage — global (outside VPC, accessed via S3 VPC Endpoint)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How is tenant data isolated in the S3 bucket using prefix policies?')
sendPrompt('How does KMS encryption work for S3 and RDS in this project?')
